title: Storage buffers, array indexing, global invocation ID
goal: Add storage buffer types (buffer[T], readonly_buffer[T], writeonly_buffer[T]) as kernel parameters, enable array indexing (buf[i] read/write), and expose a global invocation ID builtin. Output is still codegen + spirv-val (no GPU execution — M1.5). After this milestone, saxpy-shaped kernels can be expressed and validated.
scope_in: New types in source: buffer[T], readonly_buffer[T], writeonly_buffer[T] for T in {i32, u32, i64, u64, f32, f64}
Kernel parameters: fn foo(a: readonly_buffer[f32], y: buffer[f32]) -> void
Parameter binding model: descriptor set 0, binding index assigned left-to-right (param[0] = binding 0, etc.); documented

Global invocation ID builtin: gid(axis: u32) -> u32 where axis in {0,1,2}; lowered to OpLoad of GlobalInvocationId + OpCompositeExtract(axis). Convenience: gid_x() = gid(0), gid_y() = gid(1), gid_z() = gid(2) — decide whether to expose these or require explicit gid(0)
Array indexing read: `let x: f32 = buf[i];` → OpAccessChain + OpLoad with StorageBuffer storage class
Array indexing write: `buf[i] = x;` → OpAccessChain + OpStore (requires buffer OR writeonly_buffer, rejects readonly)
Mutation rule: writes to readonly_buffer produce HirError::WriteToReadonlyBuffer; reads from writeonly_buffer produce HirError::ReadFromWriteonlyBuffer
Index type: must be u32 or u64 (i32/i64 with non-negative compile-time constant accepted); floats rejected

SPIR-V codegen: OpTypeStruct with runtime array member (SSBO pattern), OpTypePointer StorageBuffer, OpVariable StorageBuffer at global scope, OpDecorate Block on the struct (SPIR-V 1.3+ uses Block not BufferBlock), OpDecorate DescriptorSet 0 + Binding N, OpDecorate NonWritable on readonly, OpDecorate NonReadable on writeonly, OpMemberDecorate Offset 0 on the runtime array, OpDecorate ArrayStride on the runtime array
SPIR-V capability: StorageBuffer access via OpCapability Shader is sufficient for Vulkan 1.1+ SPIR-V 1.3; no extra capability required

Interface list on OpEntryPoint: all StorageBuffer variables referenced by the kernel are included per SPIR-V 1.4+ spec — but we target 1.3 where only Input/Output must be listed, so StorageBuffer is NOT in the interface list (confirm per 1.3 spec §2.17)
Example: examples/saxpy.axc — `fn saxpy(a: f32, x: readonly_buffer[f32], y: buffer[f32]) -> void { let i: u32 = gid(0); y[i] = a * x[i] + y[i]; }`
Example: examples/vector_add.axc — `fn vadd(a: readonly_buffer[f32], b: readonly_buffer[f32], c: writeonly_buffer[f32]) -> void { let i: u32 = gid(0); c[i] = a[i] + b[i]; }`
scope_out: Shared memory / workgroup-local arrays (M1.3 or later)
Dynamically-sized arrays other than runtime-array SSBOs (never — SPIR-V restricts)
Sized arrays as function-local variables (array[T, N] per DESIGN.md — M1.3)

Scalar kernel parameters (uniform buffers / push constants) — scalar param `a: f32` is in saxpy example, but pass-by-value scalar kernel params need a design choice: push constant vs uniform buffer vs specialization constant. Scope for M1.2: accept scalar parameters lowered to a single-member struct in PushConstant storage class, OR defer to M1.2b if it blows scope
acceptance_criteria: spirv-val passes on emitted SPIR-V for examples/saxpy.axc and examples/vector_add.axc
HIR rejects writes to readonly_buffer with WriteToReadonlyBuffer
HIR rejects reads from writeonly_buffer with ReadFromWriteonlyBuffer
HIR rejects float or negative-signed indices
Codegen produces exactly one OpTypeStruct per distinct T, reused across all buffer[T] params
Descriptor set = 0, binding = param-position (0, 1, 2, ...) consistent across emissions
Regression: all M0+M1.1 tests still pass (157 from prior milestone)
open_questions_for_architect: Scalar kernel parameters: push-constant, uniform buffer, or specialization constant? Or defer entirely to M1.2b?
gid() convenience aliases gid_x/y/z or require gid(0)? Parent AXIOM uses bare identifiers; AXIOM-Compute should be explicit.
StorageBuffer on interface list: SPIR-V 1.3 vs 1.4 diverge. Confirm 1.3 behavior and test.
ArrayStride decoration value: element size in bytes (4 for f32/i32, 8 for f64/i64). Confirm alignment rules per Vulkan spec std430.
